dispositions.rationale: Hard signals: provider_count=43, entity_classification=large_group, and parent_company=ADVOCATE HEALTH with da_subsidiary_iusa 440344185 repeated across the roster, plus a shared institutional domain doctors.advocatehealth.com reaching 11 locations. Rule 1 (provider_count>=8 / large_group) forbids any T1 accept, and the Advocate Health corporate/institutional parent + 11-location shared domain is exactly the corporate-control signal that must go to the adversarial verifier rather than merge as solo. The researcher's own-branded-domain solo story may reflect a genuine practice inside an Advocate address cluster, but that (or a T6 institutional call) is the verifier's job, not a merge-as-T1.

Hard signals: provider_count=3 (HOEPFNER, JACOBSON, TURNWALD) and entity_classification=small_group; notably none of the three bridged surnames matches the AO/owner Aristodemo-Paul. The researcher asserts from the About-Us page that only Dr. Aristodemo-Paul is a dentist and the rest are hygienists/front-desk, but that was not verified per-NPI, so under Rule 1 the fail-closed outcome is to correct the pristine-solo T1 down to a single-location dentist-owned group rather than accept. Shared phone across the AO's own locations is consistent with one practice, so no corporate escalation is warranted.

The researcher's high-confidence boutique-solo claim for Dr. Alhakim directly contradicts the 51-provider location bundle, and there is no corporate/DSO/EIN signal to route to the DSO verifier, so this is contradictory/unverifiable at the location grain and must not merge as-is.

Even if the researcher is right that the 'Park Place' name-match is a false positive (that brand is downstate Smile Brands), Rules 2/3/6 require hold_dso_verify whenever a shared corporate EIN cluster is present — the 5-location shared TIN is an unexplained corporate-cluster signal, and provider_count=2 shows this is not a pristine solo. Never promote to a DSO tier here; route to the adversarial pass.
